ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Haydari exists; closest parent is yogurt-based dip food product (FOODON_03301710).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Haydari; closest parent is plain yoghurt category A00LC. Haydari is a strained yogurt preparation with herbs and garlic.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Haydari or a sufficiently close yogurt dip concept.

serving.description: 1 serving as a dip or meze
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Haydari as a meze or dip is approximately 100 g, served chilled in a small bowl alongside flatbread or crudités.

nutrient_source.title: Full-fat plain yogurt, strained (Greek-style), with herbs and olive oil — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Yogurt, plain, whole milk (FDC ID 171284) and Olive oil (FDC ID 171413), adjusted for herb and garlic additions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry for Haydari exists. Values estimated from USDA SR Legacy whole-milk plain yogurt (FDC ID 171284) as the dominant base (~85% by weight), with olive oil (~8%), garlic (~3%), fresh dill (~2%), and fresh mint (~2%) contributions calculated proportionally. Strained yogurt has higher protein and fat and lower carbohydrate than unstrained; values adjusted accordingly using USDA SR Legacy Greek-style yogurt data (FDC ID 2047693) as the primary base.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of dairy foods and yogurt-based products — analogous estimate from plain whole-milk yogurt GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Haydari found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 based on plain whole-milk yogurt (GI ~36, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant ingredient. Strained yogurt has a similar or slightly lower GI due to higher protein content and lower lactose per gram. Herb and garlic additions do not meaningfully alter GI. GL per 100 g serving = (35 × 5.5) / 100 = 1.9, classified as low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~180 mg per 100 g) from added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size as the added salt in Haydari contributes approximately 180 mg sodium per 100 g; a 100 g serving represents about 8% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit, and salt should be reduced in home preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and high protein content from strained yogurt
type2_diabetes.guidance: Haydari has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of under 2 per 100 g serving, making it a suitable dip option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions; the high protein content of strained yogurt further attenuates postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Olive oil provides monounsaturated fats; moderate saturated fat from dairy
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The olive oil component of Haydari contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fatty acids (~3.5 g per 100 g), while saturated fat from the yogurt base (~3.8 g per 100 g) is moderate; individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume Haydari in reasonable portions as part of a Mediterranean-style dietary pattern, which is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Haydari is a traditional Turkish yogurt-based dip and meze dish made from thick strained yogurt (similar to Greek yogurt or süzme yoğurt) blended with fresh herbs such as dill and mint, crushed garlic, olive oil, and salt. It is served chilled and is a staple of Turkish meze spreads, typically accompanied by flatbread, pita, or raw vegetables. The texture is dense and creamy due to the straining process that removes excess whey, resulting in a higher protein and lower carbohydrate content compared to unstrained yogurt. The flavour profile is tangy, garlicky, and herbaceous, with a richness contributed by the olive oil drizzled on top. Nutritionally, Haydari provides approximately 130 kcal per 100 g, with 7.5 g protein, 8.5 g fat (predominantly from dairy and olive oil), and only 5.5 g carbohydrate, giving it a low glycemic index of approximately 35. It is a good source of calcium (~130 mg per 100 g), vitamin B12, and riboflavin from the dairy base. The olive oil component contributes monounsaturated fatty acids beneficial for cardiovascular health. Haydari is naturally gluten-free, vegetarian, and halal. It is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI and high protein content. Sodium content (~180 mg per 100 g) should be noted by those managing hypertension. In Singapore, Haydari is served at Middle Eastern and Turkish restaurants as part of a meze platter.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Haydari was found in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia, FAO/INFOODS SEA) or in USDA FoodData Central as a named dish. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for strained whole-milk yogurt as the dominant base, with proportional adjustments for olive oil, garlic, and herb contributions. GI was estimated by analogy from plain whole-milk yogurt in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 2047693 for Greek-style strained yogurt as primary base, FDC ID 171413 for olive oil) with proportional ingredient weighting. No direct laboratory analysis of Haydari was available in any approved source. 2. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on plain whole-milk yogurt (GI ~36) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Haydari was found. 3. Sodium value of 180 mg per 100 g reflects typical added salt in restaurant preparation; home-prepared versions may vary. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly reported for Haydari as a composite dish; these values carry higher uncertainty. 5. This record was sourced from field survey data (Sahara Bar & Restaurant, 58 Boat Quay) as a seed record; the field survey source was not used for nutrient values. 6. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided in accordance with the Singapore cuisine multilingual rule applied to Middle Eastern dishes served in Singapore. 7. The food is classified as Middle Eastern / Turkish in origin; the Singapore multilingual naming rule is applied because this dish is served in Singapore and the input source is field survey data.
